seo.title: Quartz, Granite & Quartzite Countertops in Spokane | Cabinets Plus
seo.description: In-house countertop fabrication and installation in Spokane. Walk through real slabs of quartz, granite, marble, and quartzite at our Sprague Avenue showroom. Free in-home measurement.

blocks.heading: Spokane's Countertop Showroom & Fabrication Shop
blocks.subtext: Quartz, granite, marble, and quartzite — fabricated and installed right here in Spokane. Visit our showroom, hand-pick your slab, and let our team handle the rest from measure to install.

blocks.title: A Wide Selection of Countertops for Kitchens, Bathrooms & Beyond
blocks.paragraphs: We carry a full range of quartz, granite, marble, and quartzite — for kitchen counters, bathroom vanities, laundry rooms, bar tops, fireplace surrounds, you name it. You can pick from our own line of manufactured quartz slabs, or browse natural stone from the trusted partners we've worked with for years. Either way, you're seeing the actual slab in person before we cut it. Whether you're tearing out old laminate or starting fresh in a new build, we'll help you find the right material for the room and the way you live in it.

blocks.title: From Slab to Sink Cutout — How We Do It

steps.title: Browse & Pick Your Slab
steps.description: Stop by the showroom and see what's in stock. For natural stone, you'll choose your exact slab in person so you know precisely how it'll look in your kitchen.

steps.title: Free In-Home Measure
steps.description: We come to you with laser measuring tools and template your space down to the millimeter — sink cutouts, cooktop openings, overhangs, the works.

steps.title: Fabrication in Our Spokane Shop
steps.description: Your slab heads to our on-site fabrication facility, where we cut, polish, and shape your edge profile. No middlemen, no shipping delays — we control the whole job.

steps.title: Professional Installation
steps.description: Our installers deliver, level, and seal your countertops in a single day for most projects. We clean up, walk you through care tips, and you're ready to use them that night.

blocks.title: Quartz, Granite, Marble, Quartzite — How to Pick the Right One
blocks.paragraphs:
Choosing a countertop isn't just about looks. It's about how the surface fits into your daily life. If you've got a busy household with kids, dogs, and a slow-cooker permanently parked on the counter, quartz is hard to beat — it's non-porous, stain-resistant, and never needs sealing. Want a one-of-a-kind look that doubles as a statement piece? Granite delivers natural patterns no other slab will ever match, plus heat resistance that handles a hot pan straight off the stove. Marble is for people who want timeless elegance and don't mind a little patina building character over time — it's gorgeous in bathrooms and baking stations. And quartzite is the sweet spot: marble's airy white-and-gray look with durability close to granite.

Spokane's dry summers and cold winters don't really affect indoor stone, but our hard local water can leave mineral spots on natural stone if it isn't sealed properly — something we always handle as part of our install. Whatever direction you lean, our team will give you straight answers about maintenance, longevity, and price so you can make a decision you'll be happy with for the next 20 years.

blocks.heading: Find Your Perfect Countertop
blocks.subtext: With a huge selection of premium quartz and granite, sourced from trusted partners, Cabinets Plus brings you top-quality countertops to match any style and space.

faqs.question: How much do countertops cost in Spokane?
faqs.answer: Pricing depends on material, square footage, and edge details. In Spokane, quartz typically runs $50–$95 per square foot installed, granite $45–$90, and quartzite or premium marble $80–$150+. We provide free, accurate quotes based on a real measurement of your space — no rough phone estimates.

faqs.question: Do you fabricate countertops in-house, or do you outsource?
faqs.answer: Everything is fabricated at our Spokane facility. Cutting, polishing, edge work, sink cutouts — it all happens here. That's why we can hit tight timelines and catch issues before they ever leave the shop.

faqs.question: What's the difference between quartz and quartzite?
faqs.answer: Quartz is engineered stone (about 90% natural quartz crushed and bound with resin), while quartzite is 100% natural stone formed from sandstone under heat and pressure. Quartz is non-porous and never needs sealing; quartzite is harder than granite but does require periodic sealing.

faqs.question: How long does countertop installation take?
faqs.answer: After you choose your slab, the typical turnaround is 1–2 weeks from measure to install. Installation day itself usually takes 4–6 hours depending on kitchen size. We work around your schedule whenever possible.

faqs.question: Do I need to seal my granite or marble countertops?
faqs.answer: Yes — natural stone (granite, marble, quartzite) needs sealing about once a year to prevent staining. We seal everything before install and walk you through how to test and reapply sealer down the road. Quartz never needs sealing.

faqs.question: Can I bring in my own slab or one from another supplier?
faqs.answer: We focus on stones we source from our trusted suppliers and our own manufactured quartz line, because we can stand behind the quality and grain. If you've found something specific elsewhere, talk to us — we may already carry it or have access to the same supplier.

faqs.question: Do you serve areas outside of Spokane?
faqs.answer: Yes. We regularly install countertops throughout Spokane County and into North Idaho — including Spokane Valley, Liberty Lake, Cheney, Deer Park, Coeur d'Alene, Post Falls, and Hayden.

faqs.question: Will my old countertops be removed and disposed of?
faqs.answer: Yes, removal and disposal of your existing countertops is part of our installation service. We protect your cabinets and floors during demo, haul everything away, and leave the space ready for the new tops.
